source: Incus Server - 4 Firewall Setup: Setting up firewalld zones for the bridge, local, and trusted networks

questions.q: Create a new permanent firewalld zone called bridge.
questions.why: A custom zone keeps the bridge rules clear; --permanent writes it to disk.

questions.q: After adding a zone, what must you do?
questions.why: firewall-cmd --reload activates the newly added zone.

questions.q: To allow ALL traffic on an interface without listing services/protocols, you must...
options: Change the zone's target from default to ACCEPT
questions.why: Setting --set-target=ACCEPT allows everything on that zone's interface/source.

questions.q: Changing a firewalld zone's target must be done with which flag?
questions.why: The book notes target changes must use --permanent.

questions.q: How do you attach the incusbr0 bridge to the bridge zone?
questions.why: --add-interface binds the interface to the zone.

questions.q: How do you allow SSH only from the trusted LAN (192.168.1.0/24)?
options: Add the source to the trusted zone and add the ssh service: --zone=trusted --add-source=192.168.1.0/24 then --add-service=ssh
questions.why: The built-in trusted zone (target ACCEPT) plus the LAN source and the ssh service restricts SSH to the LAN.

questions.q: For security, what does the book have you remove from the public zone (after confirming LAN access)?
questions.why: SSH is removed from public so it is only reachable from the trusted LAN -- verify access first to avoid a lockout.
